title: PsyClaw Flowchart Protocol
description: Unified protocol for save, import, and render operations between chatbot and flowchart editor

FLOWCHART_GENERATED.description: Message sent from chatbot to parent when AI generates flowchart data

content.description: Generated flowchart JSON content as string

fileName.description: Suggested file name for the generated flowchart

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the flowchart was generated

SAVE_PROJECT.description: Command to save the current flowchart project

projectData.description: The complete project data to save

IMPORT_PROJECT.description: Command to import a flowchart project

file.description: File object from File System Access API

RENDER_FLOWCHART.description: Command to render a flowchart in the editor

autoLayout.description: Whether to auto-calculate positions for routines

PROJECT_SAVED.description: Response when project is successfully saved

fileName.description: The file name that was saved

PROJECT_IMPORTED.description: Response when project is successfully imported

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 datetime when project was created/updated

Routine.description: A routine representing a single trial/stimulus presentation. Routines are located at even-numbered points (2, 4, 6, 8...) on the flowchart canvas. The point number can be calculated as: pointNumber = (routineId * 2) + 2

id.description: Unique routine identifier (0, 2, 4, 6...). Even numbers only, corresponding to even points (2, 4, 6, 8...)

Component.description: A stimulus or response component in a routine

code.description: Python/JS code to execute (for code components)

Loop.description: A loop/trial handler that repeats routines. Loops are located at odd-numbered points (1, 3, 5, 7...) on the flowchart canvas. The point number can be calculated as: pointNumber = (loopId * 2) + 1. Loop connects from startPoint (left side) to endPoint (right side).

id.description: Unique loop identifier (0, 1, 2, 3...). Loop IDs map to odd points (1, 3, 5, 7...)

startPoint.description: The point number where the loop starts (left side). Must be an odd number (1, 3, 5, 7...). This is the loop entry point.

endPoint.description: The point number where the loop ends (right side). Must be an odd number (1, 3, 5, 7...). This is the loop exit point. If endPoint < startPoint, the loop goes backward (repeating routines).

conditions.description: JSON string representing condition objects array

isTrials.description: Whether this is a trial handler

pointSystem.description: Point numbering: points start from 1. ODD points (1, 3, 5, 7...) are loop connection points. EVEN points (2, 4, 6, 8...) are routine locations.
pointSystem.example: Routine 0 at point 2, Routine 2 at point 4, Routine 4 at point 6. Loop connects from odd point to odd point (e.g., point 1 to point 5).

steps: 1. Collect current state from flowchart editor (routines, loops, connections)
2. Transform connections (with point labels) to loops format (with startPoint/endPoint)
3. Build ProjectData object with version 2.0+
4. Convert to JSON string with pretty printing
5. Trigger file download or write to file handle
6. Send 'project-saved' response message

dataTransform.connectionsToLoops: Convert internal connection format (with point labels) to standardized loop format (with startPoint/endPoint)

pointSystem.description: Point numbering: points start from 1. ODD points (1, 3, 5, 7...) are loop connection points. EVEN points (2, 4, 6, 8...) are routine locations.
pointSystem.example: Routine 0 at point 2, Routine 2 at point 4, Routine 4 at point 6. Loop connects from odd point to odd point (e.g., point 1 to point 5).

steps: 1. Open file picker dialog (File System Access API or fallback)
2. Read and parse JSON file
3. Validate project data structure
4. Call parseProjectData to normalize format (support v1 and v2)
5. Clear current project state
6. Load routines and loops into editor
7. Recalculate routine heights based on components
8. Send 'project-imported' response message

dataTransform.parseProjectData: Normalize old (v1) and new (v2+) formats to internal format
dataTransform.loopsToConnections: Convert loop format (with startPoint/endPoint) to internal connection format (with point labels)

pointSystem.description: Point numbering: points start from 1. ODD points (1, 3, 5, 7...) are loop connection points. EVEN points (2, 4, 6, 8...) are routine locations.
pointSystem.example: Routine 0 at point 2, Routine 2 at point 4, Routine 4 at point 6. Loop connects from odd point to odd point (e.g., point 1 to point 5).

steps: 1. Receive 'flowchart-generated' message from chatbot iframe
2. Parse JSON content from message
3. Apply JSON fixes for common format issues (trailing commas, unquoted keys, single quotes)
4. Validate JSON structure (check for routines/loops arrays)
5. Clear current project state
6. Import routines with auto-calculated positions (if autoLayout enabled)
7. Transform components to avtpComponents format
8. Import loops and convert to connections (startPoint/endPoint to point labels)
9. Recalculate all routine heights
10. Redraw canvas
11. Update UI state (selected routine, form values)

dataTransform.componentsToAvtpComponents: Transform AI-generated components to internal avtpComponents format
dataTransform.autoLayout: Calculate X/Y positions for routines (horizontal layout, 250px spacing)
dataTransform.pointCalculation: Routines are placed at even points (2, 4, 6...). Loops connect odd points (1, 3, 5...)

v1Format.description: Legacy format used in older project files
